libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use an in-memory JDBC URL such as `jdbc:otel:h2:mem:otel_jdbc_test;DB_CLOSE_DELAY=-1;DB_CLOSE_ON_EXIT=FALSE` for the driver path, and/or wrap `org.h2.jdbcx.JdbcDataSource` with `JdbcTelemetry.create(OpenTelemetry.noop()).wrap(...)` for the DataSource path. Call `OpenTelemetryDriver.install(OpenTelemetry.noop())` before obtaining driver connections. No Docker service or environment variable is needed.

deterministic_setup.reason: OpenTelemetry JDBC wraps a real JDBC Driver/DataSource; H2 provides an embedded in-memory JDBC implementation so tests can exercise OpenTelemetryDriver, JdbcTelemetry.wrap(...), connections, statements, prepared statements, and result sets without an external database.

risks: The OpenTelemetry JDBC driver registers globally with DriverManager; tests should avoid deregistering unrelated drivers or leaking global state between test methods.
No-op OpenTelemetry is enough for wrapper/native reachability coverage, but it will not assert emitted spans unless the generated test adds SDK/test exporter infrastructure.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The artifact is only an OpenTelemetry wrapper for JDBC and has no concrete JDBC driver dependency; meaningful coverage requires an underlying JDBC implementation. H2 in-memory is sufficient and matches the library documentation/examples and upstream test setup without requiring Docker.
